title: Japan’s Economy Shrinks, but Outlook Is Brighter as Virus Ebbs
content: The coronavirus and supply chain issues stymied growth in the third quarter, but high vaccination rates and low numbers of virus cases point to a recovery.

more_info.byline: By Ben Dooley and Hisako Ueno

en_title: Japan’s Economy Shrinks, but Outlook Is Brighter as Virus Ebbs
en_content: The coronavirus and supply chain issues stymied growth in the third quarter, but high vaccination rates and low numbers of virus cases point to a recovery.

title: On Podcasts and Radio, Misleading Covid-19 Talk Goes Unchecked
content: False statements about vaccines have spread on the “Wild West” of media, even as some hosts die of virus complications.

title: As Holidays Near, Bosses Try to Coax Vietnam’s Workers Back to Factories
content: Vietnam, one of the world’s largest suppliers of apparel and footwear, is experiencing a labor shortage. Many employees are reluctant to return after a harsh summer lockdown.

more_info.byline: By Sui-Lee Wee and Vo Kieu Bao Uyen

en_title: As Holidays Near, Bosses Try to Coax Vietnam’s Workers Back to Factories
en_content: Vietnam, one of the world’s largest suppliers of apparel and footwear, is experiencing a labor shortage. Many employees are reluctant to return after a harsh summer lockdown.

title: Peace Corps Volunteers Fall Through the Cracks of a Student Loan Fix
content: A deserving group of dedicated people has been left out of the government’s latest patch for the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program.
en_title: Peace Corps Volunteers Fall Through the Cracks of a Student Loan Fix

title: Kaiser Permanente Reaches Tentative Deal With Unions, Averting Strike
content: More than 30,000 West Coast workers at the health care provider had planned to go on strike on Monday.

title: Deere Reaches Third Tentative Deal With Union in Attempt to End Strike
content: About 10,000 workers primarily in Iowa and Illinois have been out since Oct. 14. They rejected two previous contract proposals negotiated by the United Automobile Workers.
en_title: Deere Reaches Third Tentative Deal With Union in Attempt to End Strike
en_content: About 10,000 workers primarily in Iowa and Illinois have been out since Oct. 14. They rejected two previous contract proposals negotiated by the United Automobile Workers.

title: People Magazine Heads to New Ownership. Again.

title: Trump to Sell Marquee Washington Hotel for at Least $375 Million

title: ‘Ghost Guns’: Firearm Kits Bought Online Fuel Epidemic of Violence
content: They are untraceable, assembled from parts and can be ordered by gang members, felons and even children. They are increasingly the lethal weapon of easy access around the U.S., but especially California.

org_facet: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives

en_title: ‘Ghost Guns’: Firearm Kits Bought Online Fuel Epidemic of Violence
en_content: They are untraceable, assembled from parts and can be ordered by gang members, felons and even children. They are increasingly the lethal weapon of easy access around the U.S., but especially California.

title: Donors Withhold Gifts to Protest Changes at Hamptons Sculpture Garden
content: Some patrons of the LongHouse Reserve are upset that its veteran director was let go, but the board says it needed new leadership to expand the garden into a museum with a broader mission.

title: AstraZeneca says it will start profiting from its vaccine.

title: Appeals Court Extends Block on Biden’s Vaccine Mandate for Employers
content: A three-judge panel of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit held that challengers were likely to succeed in their claim that the mandate was an unlawful overreach.
